name: Ubiquiti UniFi Switch Hi-Capacity Aggregation

description: The UniFi Switch Hi-Capacity Aggregation (USW-Pro-Aggregation) is a fully managed Layer 3 aggregation switch engineered for enterprise and data-center environments...

detailed_description.heading: High-Capacity Aggregation Switching
detailed_description.text: The UniFi Switch Hi-Capacity Aggregation (USW-Pro-Aggregation) is a fully managed Layer 3 aggregation switch engineered for enterprise and data-center environments that demand exceptional throughput and port density. It features a powerful combination of 28 × 10G SFP+ ports and 4 × 25G SFP28 ports, allowing network architects to consolidate access switches, servers, and high-performance uplinks into a single aggregation layer. This port layout supports flexible speed negotiation, making it suitable for mixed-generation network infrastructures.

detailed_description.heading: Layer 3 Capabilities
detailed_description.text: The USW-Pro-Aggregation goes beyond traditional aggregation switches by offering advanced Layer 3 functionality, enabling more efficient and simplified network designs. With inter-VLAN routing for local networks, traffic between VLANs can be handled directly on the switch, reducing reliance on external routers and lowering overall network latency. This approach improves performance while simplifying network topology. The switch supports a DHCP server for local networks, allowing administrators to assign IP addresses without additional infrastructure. For larger or distributed environments, DHCP relay ensures seamless integration with centralized DHCP services. Static routing for local networks enables controlled traffic paths between network segments, providing predictable behavior and easier troubleshooting. These Layer 3 features allow the USW-Pro-Aggregation to function as a powerful distribution or collapsed-core switch. By handling routing tasks closer to the edge, organizations can reduce complexity, improve response times, and optimize bandwidth utilization. The result is a cleaner, more efficient network architecture that scales smoothly as demands increase, while remaining easy to manage within the UniFi Network platform.

detailed_description.heading: Advanced Layer 2 Features
detailed_description.text: The USW-Pro-Aggregation includes an extensive suite of Layer 2 features designed to deliver granular control, enhanced security, and optimized traffic handling. LACP port aggregation enables higher bandwidth and link redundancy, while STP and RSTP protect the network from loops and ensure stability. QoS with DSCP support allows prioritization of latency-sensitive traffic such as voice and video. For professional audiovisual deployments, the switch offers Pro AV profiles, including support for technologies such as Dante, Q-SYS, NDI, SDVoE, Shure, AES67, and Crestron. These profiles simplify configuration and ensure consistent performance for demanding real-time media applications. Advanced multicast handling is provided through IGMP Snooping, IGMP Querier, and Fast Leave, ensuring efficient distribution of multicast streams. Security and traffic control are reinforced with 802.1X authentication, MAC- and IP-based ACLs, device isolation, DHCP snooping, storm control, and rate limiting for broadcast and multicast traffic. Additional tools such as port mirroring, jumbo frames, LLDP-MED, Voice VLAN, and loop protection make the switch adaptable to complex enterprise scenarios.

detailed_description.heading: Reliable Power and Centralized Management
detailed_description.text: Designed for mission-critical environments, the USW-Pro-Aggregation features a 1U rack-mount form factor compatible with racks ranging from 400 to 1200 mm in depth. Its enclosure and mounting components are constructed from durable SGCC steel, ensuring mechanical strength and long-term reliability. A built-in 1.3-inch touchscreen display provides instant access to device status and key operational information. The switch supports DC power backup via USP RPS, adding redundancy for enhanced uptime in case of primary power failure. It uses an internal AC/DC power supply with universal input and a maximum power consumption of 100 W. Thermal efficiency is carefully engineered, with heat dissipation of 341 BTU/hr, supporting stable operation across a wide range of environmental conditions. Management is handled through the UniFi Network application (version 6.1.25 and later), providing centralized configuration, monitoring, and firmware updates. With NDAA compliance and certifications including CE, FCC, IC, and Anatel, the USW-Pro-Aggregation meets the requirements of enterprise, government, and regulated deployments.

items.name: Power Method
items.value: AC/DC Adapter, USP RPS DC input
